seo.title: Lamb ribs - over the coals - Marinda Kook

html:
The ribs need to be cooked slowly and unhurriedly over gentle coals. More than one glass of wine should be drunk. When you take the ribs off, they should be charred – almost as if they'd burnt. That's where the deep deliciousness lives.

YOU'LL NEED:

  • 2 pieces of lamb or mutton rib
  • 2 sprigs of fresh rosemary
  • 6 garlic cloves
  • 250 ml tomato sauce
  • 125 ml Worcester sauce
  • 125 ml chutney
  • 60 ml vinegar
  • 60 ml brown sugar
  • a generous amount of coriander
  • salt and pepper

NOW:

  1. season the meat generously with coriander, salt and pepper
  2. chop the rosemary and garlic finely and put in a bowl
  3. add the rest of the ingredientsand rub it well into the meat
  4. leave it to marinate in the fridge for at least 60 minutes
  5. get your coals ready and braai slowly, not too low.
  6. even if it looks like it's burning, don't panic, that's the delicious part

Eat the meat with the traditional braaibroodjie and tomato salad.
